name: fltk-rs
description: Rust bindings for FLTK 1.4 — lightweight, statically-linkable cross-platform GUI toolkit with ~80 widgets and tiny binaries

license.comment: MIT licensed. The underlying FLTK library itself uses a permissive LGPL-with-static-linking exception, but the fltk-rs crate is MIT.

latest-stable-version.comment: Latest stable release on crates.io as of research date.

maintenance-status.comment: 289 releases, latest 1.5.22 on 2025-11-16; repo updated March 2026 per search snapshot.

maturity.comment: Post-1.0 (now 1.5.x) with 5+ years of releases; API considered stable by maintainers.

corporate-backing.comment: Primarily maintained by Mohammed Alyousef (MoAlyousef); started for a university hospital simulation center use case. No corporate sponsor.

implementation.comment: Wraps the FLTK 1.4 C++ library via a cfltk C wrapper + fltk-sys bindings; C++17 compiler required at build time.

rendering-mode.comment: Classic retained widget tree; widgets persist and redraw on demand.

reactivity-model.comment: Traditional callback/event-handler model, with an optional message-passing channel API (crossbeam-channel) on top.

thread-model.comment: Event handling and drawing must occur on the main thread; worker threads communicate via app::awake_callback or channels, with an optional app::lock/unlock for widget mutation.

renderer.comment: Default renderer is FLTK's own CPU-based drawing via native OS graphics (GDI on Windows, Quartz/Cocoa on macOS, X11/Cairo or Wayland on Linux). OpenGL available through GlWindow with the `enable-glwindow` feature.

gpu-acceleration.comment: UI is not GPU-accelerated by default. OpenGL windows can be opted into for custom rendering, but the standard widget pipeline is CPU.

hidpi-support.comment: FLTK 1.4 (which fltk-rs 1.5.x wraps) introduced screen-specific scale factors and retina support; Ctrl+/-/0 shortcuts adjust scaling at runtime.

vector-rendering.comment: FLTK provides vector-drawing primitives (lines, arcs, Bezier paths) and includes an SVG image widget.

custom-shaders.comment: Only within a GlWindow using the `enable-glwindow` feature; you must manage OpenGL shaders yourself via glow or similar. Not part of the standard widget renderer.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: GlWindow lets you embed OpenGL-rendered content. No built-in wgpu adapter.

linux-wayland.comment: Supported via the `use-wayland` feature flag (FLTK hybrid Wayland/X11 backend). CFLTK_WAYLAND_ONLY env var allows Wayland-only builds.

ios.comment: Not listed as an official target.

android.comment: Not listed as an official target.

web-wasm.comment: No WASM/browser target; library is statically linked native C++ code.

embedded-no-std.comment: Requires std and a C++17 runtime; not targeted at no_std or microcontrollers.

authoring-style.comment: Builder-style imperative Rust API. Optional DSL-ish path via fl2rust which converts FLUID `.fl` UI files to Rust code at build time, but there is no native markup language.

hot-reload.comment: No runtime hot reload; UI changes require recompilation.

layout-system.comment: Provides Flex (flexbox-style), Pack, and manual coordinate layout built-in; Grid widget available via the separate fltk-grid crate.

styling-approach.comment: Five built-in color schemes (Base, GTK, Plastic, Gleam, Oxy) selected via an app-wide API; per-widget properties (color, font, box type) set imperatively. Additional themes via the fltk-theme crate.

theme-switching.comment: Scheme and widget colors can be changed at runtime via app::set_scheme and redraw; fltk-theme supports light/dark presets.

accessibility.comment: Integration exists via the separate fltk-accesskit crate (AccessKit adapter). Not built into the core crate, and coverage depends on which widgets the developer registers with AccessibilityContext.

internationalization.comment: RTL and CJK text shaping rely on Pango (default on Linux). The `no-pango` feature flag removes it at the cost of losing RTL/CJK support. No built-in locale/translation system — users wire their own.

widget-breadth.comment: ~80 widgets including buttons, inputs, menus, tables (Fl_Table), trees, browsers, text display/editor, valuators, dialogs, and an SVG image widget. Missing first-party: rich-text/markdown and charts/plotting (both require third-party crates).

data-grid-table.comment: Base Fl_Table widget ships with fltk; fltk-table provides a smart wrapper with reduced boilerplate. Virtualized row drawing via draw_cell callback.

rich-text-markdown.comment: TextEditor/TextDisplay widgets support styled text (style tables) but there is no first-party markdown or rich-text widget.

native-menus.comment: MenuBar, MenuButton, Choice, and SysMenuBar (macOS native menu bar) all provided.

file-dialogs.comment: Both FLTK-drawn FileChooser and platform-native NativeFileChooser (Win32, Cocoa, GTK) are built-in.

charts-plotting.comment: A legacy Chart widget exists but is very basic; no first-party modern charting. Users typically embed plotters output or use a custom widget.

custom-widgets.comment: Documented `widget_extends!` macro + dedicated book chapter with working examples (custom button, custom dial). Low-to-moderate boilerplate; needs awareness of draw/handle overrides.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: fltk-evented provides async Listener widgets usable with tokio or async-std; demos repo shows tokio + reqwest integration. No built-in executor — you pick one.

system-tray.comment: FLTK itself does not expose tray APIs; a third-party crate (e.g., tray-item) is required. A demo exists at fltk-rs/demos/systray.

notifications.comment: No native OS notification API in fltk-rs; use a separate crate (e.g., notify-rust).

clipboard.comment: app::copy / app::paste exposed; text and image clipboard supported.

drag-and-drop.comment: Dragged files surface as a Paste event with app::event_text() holding the path.

embedded-webview.comment: Not in core, but the first-party fltk-webview crate (a wry-based webview embedded in an fltk window) is maintained by the same org.

multi-window.comment: Multiple top-level Windows are a core FLTK feature.

binary-size.comment: Maintainers advertise ~1MB stripped release binary for a minimal app — unusually small for a full GUI toolkit. Not independently benchmarked here.

startup-time.comment: Maintainer describes startup as 'fast'; FLTK is well known for sub-50ms cold start. No independent measurement collected on 2026-04-24.

memory-footprint.comment: FLTK minimal apps typically sit well under 20MB RSS. Claim based on project README and general FLTK reputation, not measured on 2026-04-24.

idle-redraw.comment: FLTK only redraws widgets whose damage() bits are set; no continuous frame loop.

dependency-count.comment: Only 6 direct Rust dependencies (bitflags, crossbeam-channel, fltk-sys, minipaste, once_cell, ttf-parser). Transitive tree is small compared to wgpu-based frameworks, and most of the weight is the vendored FLTK C++ source compiled via cmake.

documentation-quality.comment: Dedicated fltk-book with tutorials, thorough docs.rs API reference, FAQ, and video series. Book self-describes as 'introductory'; advanced FLTK semantics sometimes require consulting FLTK C++ docs.

api-ergonomics.comment: Builder pattern and trait-based widget exts are idiomatic; however, the C++ lineage leaks through (widget_extends! macro, group begin/end pattern, callback Rc<RefCell> idioms). Functional and reasonably clean but not as polished as iced/Dioxus.

examples-count.comment: 30+ examples in the main repo plus a dedicated fltk-rs/demos repository with integration samples (tokio, reqwest, serde, webview, systray, opengl, etc.).

interactive-playground.comment: No online playground or demo gallery — the library runs native and cannot target WASM. Examples must be built locally.

ide-tooling.comment: No framework-specific LSP, formatter, or inspector. Relies entirely on rust-analyzer + rustfmt since authoring is plain Rust. The FLUID designer (C++ side) produces .fl files that fl2rust can convert, but FLUID itself is not an fltk-rs tool.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: No runtime widget inspector comparable to browser devtools.

github-stars.comment: Approximately 1.8k stars as of 2026-04-24 research. Snapshot will age.

crates-io-downloads.comment: lib.rs reports ~15k downloads per month but does not expose an all-time total here. Setting null rather than guessing; refresh once crates.io total is directly verified.

notable-production-users.comment: No widely-known shipping commercial apps publicly attributed to fltk-rs. Origin story references a university hospital simulation-center use case, but no marquee production user is documented on the project pages.

community-size.comment: Active GitHub Discussions and one primary maintainer with a handful of regular contributors; no dedicated Discord/Matrix. Smaller than iced/egui/Slint communities.

ecosystem-crates.comment: First-party org hosts fltk-theme, fltk-table, fltk-grid, fltk-evented, fltk-webview, fltk-accesskit, fltk-term, fl2rust, fltk-observe — a useful set of adjacent crates, though still narrower than the ecosystems around iced or Tauri.
